title: Preserve Every Cell’s Header Context
summary: Build and verify table header associations in multi-center resource matrix through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain table header associations from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply table header associations without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: multi-center resource matrix phase 1.1: identify the row and column headers for representative data cells
multi-center resource matrix phase 1.2: use th and scope when each relationship is unambiguous
multi-center resource matrix phase 1.3: add id and headers only for multi-level or irregular relationships
multi-center resource matrix phase 1.4: inspect announced headers for first, middle, empty, and total cells

steps.title: 04 · Read the multi-center resource matrix case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves table header associations when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: multi-center resource matrix.
steps.why: Preserve Every Cell’s Header Context makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in multi-center resource matrix.

content.text: Header cells create programmatic context. Scope col and scope row are clear for simple tables. Complex matrices may require stable header ids and headers references so each data cell identifies all relevant headers. Bold text or a first-row visual style cannot replace th relationships. Apply it to multi-center resource matrix.

hints: Preserve Every Cell’s Header Context checkpoint 4: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Preserve Every Cell’s Header Context checkpoint 4: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Preserve Every Cell’s Header Context checkpoint 4: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Multi-center resource matrix case 4: bold first-row cells provide the same programmatic relationship as header cells.
Multi-center resource matrix case 4: use th with scope for simple relationships and explicit id-headers mappings only when the matrix truly needs them.
Multi-center resource matrix case 4: put scope on td cells because data cells know which direction they belong to.
